name: Dify
description: Open-source LLM application and agentic workflow platform with a visual canvas, built-in RAG pipeline, agent nodes, and 50+ built-in tools. One of the most-starred LLM app platforms, deployable self-hosted or via Dify Cloud.

evidence.value: Visual workflow orchestration with RAG grounding improves task accuracy for production LLM apps

task_completion_accuracy.methodology: Task completion testing across chatflow, workflow, and agent app types

evidence.value: 50+ built-in tools plus custom API tools, plugin marketplace, and MCP support with schema validation

tool_use_reliability.methodology: Tool invocation testing across built-in, custom, and plugin tools

evidence.value: Visual canvas supports branching, iteration, parallel nodes, and agent nodes for autonomous sub-tasks

multi_step_planning.methodology: Complex workflow construction and execution testing

evidence.value: Conversation variables, session memory, and knowledge-base persistence across chat sessions

memory_persistence.methodology: Memory evaluation across sessions and knowledge bases

evidence.value: Node-level error handling with retry, fail branches, and default-value fallbacks in workflows

error_recovery.methodology: Error injection testing on workflow error branches and retries

evidence.value: Agent nodes embed autonomous strategies inside workflows, but deep multi-agent orchestration is less developed than code-first frameworks

agent_collaboration.methodology: Multi-agent pattern testing using agent nodes within workflows

evidence.value: Code nodes execute in the dedicated dify-sandbox service with syscall restrictions; external tools run via HTTP without sandboxing

tool_sandboxing.methodology: Security architecture review of code execution sandbox and tool isolation

evidence.value: Workspace member roles, app-level API keys, and SSO/access policies in premium/enterprise editions

access_control.methodology: Access control assessment of workspace roles and API key scoping

evidence.value: Built-in content moderation (keyword, OpenAI moderation, custom API) for inputs/outputs; no dedicated injection defense

prompt_injection_defense.methodology: Injection testing with moderation toolkits enabled

evidence.value: Workspace- and app-scoped data with isolated knowledge bases; self-hosting gives full infrastructure isolation

data_isolation.methodology: Data isolation architecture review across cloud and self-hosted modes

evidence.value: Fully public codebase with 138k+ stars; license is Apache-2.0-based but adds multi-tenant SaaS resale restrictions

open_source_transparency.methodology: Source code and license terms review

evidence.value: Self-hosted deployments keep all logs, conversations, and knowledge data in user-controlled databases

data_retention.methodology: Privacy architecture review of self-hosted versus cloud retention

evidence.value: Published privacy policy for cloud; self-hosting enables full GDPR data-controller compliance

gdpr_compliance.methodology: Compliance capabilities assessment across deployment modes

evidence.value: Data flows to configured model providers and any third-party tools invoked in workflows; local models supported via Ollama/Xinference

third_party_data_sharing.methodology: Data flow analysis across model providers and tool integrations

evidence.value: Free self-hosting via Docker Compose or Helm, including fully local model serving with Ollama

local_deployment_option.methodology: Deployment options assessment including air-gapped configurations

evidence.value: Extensive multilingual docs covering workflows, RAG, tools, plugins, and self-hosting

evidence.source: Dify Logs and Annotations / Tracing Integrations
evidence.value: Built-in conversation logs, node-level run traces, and integrations with LangSmith, Langfuse, and Opik
Visual canvas plus per-node inputs/outputs and citation/attribution in RAG answers make behavior inspectable

decision_explainability.methodology: Explainability assessment of visual runs and RAG citations

evidence.value: Apache-2.0-based custom license; restricts operating multi-tenant SaaS and removing branding without commercial license

open_source_code.methodology: License terms review against OSI-standard licenses

evidence.value: 138k+ GitHub stars and 1M+ deployed apps as of April 2026; very active releases and plugin marketplace

community_activity.methodology: Community engagement analysis of stars, deployments, and release cadence

evidence.value: No-code visual builder plus Backend-as-a-Service APIs; running app possible in minutes via Docker or Cloud

ease_of_integration.methodology: Integration complexity assessment for no-code and API usage

evidence.value: Kubernetes/Helm deployments with horizontally scalable API and worker services; 1M+ apps deployed on the platform
Free self-hosted; Cloud Sandbox free, Professional $59/mo, Team $159/mo with clear plan limits

cost_predictability.methodology: Pricing model analysis across self-hosted and cloud tiers

evidence.value: Built-in usage analytics, conversation logs, annotation workflows, and external tracing integrations
Mature 1.x platform with frequent stable releases, plugin system, and large production install base

production_readiness.methodology: Production readiness assessment of release maturity and adoption

customer-support.notes: RAG-grounded chatbots with citations, moderation, and annotation workflows are a core strength

content-creation.notes: Visual workflows with templates suit marketing and content generation pipelines

research-assistant.notes: Knowledge bases plus web/search tools support internal research assistants

data-analysis.notes: Sandboxed code nodes handle light analysis; heavy data work better in code-first frameworks

education.notes: Easy no-code building of tutoring and Q&A apps over course materials

code-generation.notes: Possible via LLM nodes but not a developer-workflow-focused platform

legal-compliance.notes: Self-hosted RAG over legal documents works, but compliance guardrails need configuration

best_for: Teams wanting no-code/low-code agentic workflows with built-in RAG
Enterprises self-hosting LLM apps for data control with a visual ops layer
Product teams shipping chatbots and assistants via Backend-as-a-Service APIs
Organizations standardizing many internal LLM apps on one platform
strengths: Visual workflow canvas combining agent nodes, RAG, and 50+ built-in tools
Massive adoption and community: 138k+ GitHub stars, 1M+ deployed apps
Free self-hosting with Docker/Helm and full data control
Built-in observability: logs, annotations, analytics, and tracing integrations
Clear, affordable cloud pricing (free Sandbox, $59/mo Professional, $159/mo Team)
limitations: License is Apache-2.0-based but custom: multi-tenant SaaS resale and logo removal require a commercial license
Multi-agent orchestration is shallower than code-first frameworks like LangGraph or CrewAI
Visual abstraction limits fine-grained programmatic control for complex agent logic
Prompt injection defense relies on opt-in moderation rather than dedicated mechanisms
Self-hosted stack (API, worker, sandbox, vector DB) has nontrivial operational footprint

metadata.license: Dify Open Source License (Apache 2.0 based, with multi-tenant SaaS resale restrictions)
supported_models: Hundreds of models via provider plugins
metadata.pricing: Free self-hosted; Cloud: Sandbox free, Professional $59/mo, Team $159/mo
metadata.adoption: 1M+ apps deployed on the platform as of April 2026
